title: Crypto Policy Pivot
one_line_summary: U.S. policy tone toward crypto may be improving, but implementation durability remains contested.
claims: Recent political and regulatory signals suggest a more accommodative stance toward crypto than in the prior cycle.
Narrative strength is rising faster than durable implementation proof.
counterpoints: Political messaging can improve faster than actual regulatory implementation.
Short-term sentiment may overstate structural policy change.
actionSignals: Monitor whether softer rhetoric converts into durable rulemaking.
Track enforcement posture change, not just speeches.

title: AI Infra Overbuild
one_line_summary: AI infrastructure spending is accelerating faster than proven monetization, strengthening the overbuild narrative.
claims: CAPEX into AI infrastructure is outpacing demonstrated downstream revenue capture.
Power and compute bottlenecks are becoming part of the same narrative cluster.
counterpoints: Infrastructure often looks excessive before applications catch up.
The market may be underestimating delayed monetization instead of overbuilding.
actionSignals: Power-linked infrastructure becoming a recurring signal
evidence: Energy-demand narratives linked to AI buildout

title: Ethereum ETF Flow Repricing
one_line_summary: The market is repricing ETH around changing ETF flow expectations and rotation logic.
claims: ETF flow expectations are becoming a stronger ETH valuation input.
Rotation logic from BTC into ETH is being re-discussed under changing macro tone.
counterpoints: Narrative may be front-running actual sustained flow evidence.
ETH-specific structural concerns may offset headline ETF optimism.
actionSignals: Institutional positioning commentary around ETH flows
Renewed allocator discussion of ETH beta and relative rotation
evidence: Positioning discussion in institutional crypto research

title: Solana Consumer Breakout
one_line_summary: Consumer-facing apps on Solana are again being framed as the chain's strongest narrative edge.
claims: Solana's strongest narrative is shifting from pure speed to consumer app traction.
Narrative energy is increasingly tied to visible user behavior, not just infra metrics.
counterpoints: Consumer spikes can remain episodic and fail to become durable platform economics.
Narrative quality may still depend on speculative bursts rather than retention.
actionSignals: Ecosystem app launches and user activity bursts
Community focus rotating toward end-user products

title: Stablecoin Payments Expansion
one_line_summary: Stablecoins are increasingly framed as the most concrete crypto utility narrative, especially in cross-border and platform payments.
claims: Stablecoins remain the clearest bridge from crypto rails into real payment usage.
The market is separating speculative token narratives from durable payment rails.
counterpoints: Regulatory and banking gatekeepers still constrain mainstream expansion.
Many payment announcements remain pilot-level rather than structural.
actionSignals: Platform integrations and payment corridor expansion
Repeated institutional messaging around settlement rails

title: DePIN Reacceleration
one_line_summary: DePIN is re-entering conversation as a hardware-linked narrative tied to AI, bandwidth, and edge infrastructure.
claims: DePIN is benefiting from adjacency to AI and infra narratives.
Hardware-linked token models are being reconsidered under new demand assumptions.
counterpoints: Many DePIN narratives overstate real adoption and understate capex complexity.
Revenue quality and token incentive sustainability remain unresolved.
actionSignals: Renewed founder and VC discussion around DePIN categories
More infra-linked project comparisons appearing in research

title: Base Ecosystem Acceleration
one_line_summary: Base is being reframed as a serious distribution and application layer rather than just another L2 narrative.
claims: Base is increasingly discussed as a distribution engine linked to Coinbase reach.
Its narrative edge comes from funnel and app surface rather than novel infra alone.
counterpoints: Narrative may overweight distribution and underweight long-term defensibility.
It still competes inside a crowded rollup field.
actionSignals: App launches and onchain campaign activity

title: Telegram x TON Consumer Rumor
one_line_summary: A weaker rumor-layer narrative that Telegram-linked consumer distribution could reignite TON usage expectations.
claims: Telegram distribution may again become a catalyst for TON-adjacent consumer flows.
Rumor energy is clustering around possible renewed user funnel narratives.
counterpoints: Most of the current energy is still narrative speculation rather than confirmed rollout.
Weak-signal chatter can create attention without durable follow-through.
actionSignals: Mostly discussion-layer chatter with limited verified execution

title: Altseason Rotation Chatter
one_line_summary: A broad low-quality market narrative suggesting imminent alt rotation, driven more by positioning chatter than durable evidence.
claims: Broader risk appetite could spill from majors into weaker beta names.
Community framing is once again clustering around altseason timing.
counterpoints: Rotation talk often appears before real liquidity breadth confirms it.
This narrative historically overpromises breadth and durability.
actionSignals: Mostly discussion-layer positioning and social chatter
